signals.title: Policy Advocacy Without Institutional Anchor
signals.evidence_anchor: Essay 1 frames the applicant as someone who 'works at the intersection of technology and public policy' - yet the activity profile shows no direct engagement with a policy body, think tank, or advocacy organisation.
signals.direct_read: The applicant self-describes a policy-facing identity, but every listed activity is either competitive (MUN, debate) or technical (coding club, hackathon). There is no activity where policy influence was an actual aim.
signals.depth_opening: Whether the applicant's policy orientation is a lived, practiced mode of engagement or a framing layer applied to competitive activity in hindsight - and if it is lived, where and how that has actually operated.
signals.why_it_matters: The answer changes whether this applicant is building toward something specific or projecting a coherent narrative onto a diffuse activity set.

signals.title: Grade Trajectory That Reverses at the Subject That Matters Most
signals.evidence_anchor: 12th grade Economics score (88%) sits below the applicant's own 11th grade score (93%) and below the class average, specifically in the subject the applicant names as their intended major.
signals.direct_read: The academic record shows a subject-specific dip in the final year, in the domain the applicant has declared as their primary direction.
signals.depth_opening: What actually happened in 12th Economics specifically - whether this reflects a change in pedagogy, syllabus difficulty, personal circumstance, or something about the subject itself that was more difficult than expected - and what the applicant's relationship to that difficulty looks like from the inside.
signals.why_it_matters: Whether the dip signals disengagement, externally caused friction, or a genuine encounter with difficulty in this domain - and how the applicant processed it - materially changes what 'interest in Economics' means for this person.

signals.title: Self-Taught Data Skill Set Disconnected From Any Visible Output
signals.evidence_anchor: The applicant lists Python, SQL, and 'data analysis' as self-acquired skills in the additional information section. The activity profile and essays contain no project, output, or application of these skills.
signals.direct_read: A stated technical skill set exists in isolation from any evidence of what it has been used to build, analyse, or produce.
signals.depth_opening: Whether these skills have been applied to anything concrete - a personal project, an analysis the applicant ran, a problem they tried to solve with data - and what that application looked like in practice, including where it broke down.
signals.why_it_matters: The presence or absence of applied output determines whether this is a functional technical capability or a listed credential. For a policy-facing direction, data skills are only informative if they have been used to understand something.

signals.title: Two-Year MUN Commitment That Produced Neither Visible Growth Nor Leadership
signals.evidence_anchor: MUN appears across 9th and 10th grade activity records with consistent participation, but no progression in role, no committee chair or secretariat position, and no essay reference.
signals.direct_read: The applicant participated in MUN for two years at the same level of involvement without apparent escalation of scope, responsibility, or reflection.
signals.depth_opening: What MUN actually gave this applicant across two years - whether it was the activity itself, the community, the specific topic areas engaged, or a context for something else - and why there was no visible push toward deeper ownership within it.
signals.why_it_matters: Two years of sustained participation without visible progression is either evidence of deliberate lateral engagement or a pattern of low-investment continuation. Understanding which one tells you something about how this applicant operates within structures over time.

signals.title: Family Background as Undeclared Shaping Force
signals.evidence_anchor: Father listed as IAS officer (senior civil servant). Essay 1 claims the applicant arrived at public policy interest 'through reading and observation'. No essay mentions family context.
signals.direct_read: The biographical context contains an obvious and direct pathway to the stated interest that the applicant has not acknowledged.
signals.depth_opening: Whether the family context has actively shaped - or actively been set aside from - the applicant's engagement with policy, and what the applicant's actual account of that relationship is.
signals.why_it_matters: The applicant's relationship to an obvious formative context - whether they are extending it, reacting against it, or genuinely operating independently of it - changes how the rest of the profile is read.

themes.title: Stated Direction vs. Practiced Engagement
themes.unifying_axis: This applicant's self-described orientation - policy, technology, public impact - is articulated with clarity but does not yet have a visible footprint of lived, applied engagement beneath it.
themes.interview_direction: Understanding where, concretely, this applicant has moved from stated interest into actual practice - and what that movement looks like when it encounters friction or requires output.

themes.title: How This Applicant Operates Within Structures Over Time
themes.unifying_axis: Across academic and activity contexts, this applicant shows a pattern of sustained participation without visible escalation - a consistency whose internal logic has not been made legible by the application.
themes.interview_direction: Understanding what sustained, level engagement means for this applicant specifically - whether it reflects deliberate choice, limited access to progression pathways, or a mode of operating that the applicant has not yet named.

themes.title: The Undeclared Context
themes.unifying_axis: A significant biographical fact - the applicant's family environment - sits entirely outside the application's self-narrative, creating an unresolved gap between what is present and what has been accounted for.
themes.interview_direction: Understanding the applicant's actual relationship to the most obvious formative context in their background, and what that relationship looks like from the inside.
